summary: Bed bug education app expanding to additional pests, with a cross-platform Photo Scanner and growing educational content to include pests like cockroaches, head lice, spiders (including black widow, brown recluse, and wolf spider guidance), moths/larvae, beetles (including wood beetles), black stink bugs, ants (black ants, red ants, army ants, and carpenter ants), and now also earwigs.
imageRequirements:
## Required Images
- Simple earwig icon illustration, clean line style, transparent background (earwig-icon.dim_128x128.png, 128x128)

existing_features.title: Add a new "Photo Scanner" feature that works on mobile and desktop browsers, allowing users on all phones to capture a photo with the camera or upload an existing image from their device.

Implement basic on-device "scan" utilities for the Photo Scanner image (client-side only), such as zoom, rotate, crop, and a simple enhancement toggle (e.g., increase contrast/sharpen-like effect using canvas processing).
Expand the educational content UI so the app includes information beyond bed bugs, covering other bugs/pests including scorpions and mice, with user-facing text in English.

Apply a cohesive visual theme refresh suitable for a "pest education + identification" app and use it consistently across the new Photo Scanner and expanded pest information sections (avoid a blue/purple-dominant palette).
Add a clear delete/trash icon control in the Photo Scanner for removing an unwanted photo from the current session (clears the selected image and resets editor state).
Implement a saved-photo gallery/backup for the Photo Scanner that persists saved scans locally in the browser and allows users to view, re-open, and delete saved items.
Add a confirmation step for destructive deletes from the saved-photo gallery to reduce accidental data loss.
Add an option in the Saved Scans gallery that lets users reorder saved scans (change the display order of saved items) and persist the updated order in localStorage.
Extend the saved scans state management hook to support reordering operations and ensure any reorder action updates both in-memory state and localStorage consistently.
Ensure the reordering UI is usable on mobile and desktop and is accessible via keyboard/screen readers (clear labels for reorder controls).
Add a clear delete option for each item in the Saved Scans gallery so users can remove saved scans, including a confirmation dialog before the delete is executed.
Extend the saved-scan data model persisted in localStorage to support user-defined tags per saved scan, while maintaining backward compatibility with already-saved scans that have no tags.
Add UI controls in the Saved Scans gallery to create, edit, and remove tags on a per-scan basis using clear, English user-facing labels.
Add a simple tag-based filtering/search capability in the Saved Scans gallery so users can quickly narrow the list to scans matching a selected tag or entered tag text.
Generate a new “high heat treatment” icon as a static image asset for the Bed Bugs issues/icons set.
Add the new “high heat treatment” icon to the Bed Bugs issues/icons UI where bed bug issues/treatment icons are presented, using the generated static asset (do not fetch via backend).

Expand the bed bug “Heat Treatment” educational content to add more detailed homeowner preparation steps to the existing “Room Preparation Checklist” (focused on prep work for the home before heat is applied), keeping all user-facing text in English.
Add an interactive checklist mode to the "Room Preparation Checklist" in the Treatment section so users can mark individual preparation tasks as completed.
Persist the Heat Treatment checklist completion state locally in the browser and provide a clear way to reset progress for the checklist.

Add a simple reminder/confirmation step before a user can start interacting with the Heat Treatment "Room Preparation Checklist" in the Treatment section, so the user explicitly acknowledges the key safety/prep reminder before checking off items.

Add a clear warning in the Heat Treatment checklist reminder (the confirmation dialog shown before users can interact with the Room Preparation Checklist) advising users not to throw out furniture or beds, because bed bugs can spread by hitchhiking and infest other homes; keep the warning text in plain English and consistent with the existing reminder dialog style.

Update the Heat Treatment checklist safety reminder dialog to include a clear, plain-English warning that users should remove all firearms and ammunition and remove/relocate any other items that could explode before heat treatment begins, while keeping the tone and formatting consistent with the existing reminder paragraphs.

Add a new item to the Heat Treatment Room Preparation Checklist (Before Treatment section) instructing users to remove all firearms and ammunition and any other items that could explode before heat is applied.

Add a new checklist item to the Heat Treatment "During Treatment" phase stating that only the heat treatment technician is allowed in the home while it is being heat treated (use clear English user-facing text).

Update the Heat Treatment checklist safety reminder dialog to include a clear warning that only the heat treatment technician is allowed in the home while the home is being heat treated, keeping the tone and formatting consistent with the existing reminder paragraphs.
Generate a new static “Quick Tip” icon image asset for the Bed Bugs educational content.

Replace the current Lucide icon in the Bed Bugs “tip” alert/callout at the top of each Bed Bugs section component with the new static “Quick Tip” icon, without changing the existing user-facing text in those alerts.
Generate a new static cockroach icon image asset and add it to the app as a static file under frontend/public/assets/generated.

Add a new Cockroaches educational content component with clear English guidance on (1) identifying common cockroaches and signs of infestation, (2) where they hide and why they appear, (3) step-by-step extermination/control options suitable for homeowners, and (4) prevention strategies to reduce future infestations.

Integrate cockroaches into the pest selection UI across the EducationalContent tabs so users can select Cockroaches and view the Cockroaches content; use the generated cockroach icon image in the Cockroaches selection control.

Add a new Head Lice educational content component with clear, user-facing English text that explains what head lice are, common signs of infestation, and practical prevention steps families can follow.
Integrate Head Lice into the pest selection UI across the EducationalContent tabs (Identify/Habitats/Prevention/Treatment) so users can select “Head Lice” and view the new Head Lice content.
Add a static Head Lice icon image under frontend/public/assets/generated and use it in the Head Lice selection control (consistent with the Cockroaches selection control pattern).

Add a new Spiders educational content component with clear, user-facing English text covering: (1) basic identification cues and common signs of spider activity indoors, (2) prevention steps to reduce spiders in/around the home, and (3) what to do if you find a spider (safe capture/removal options, cleaning steps, and when to call a professional). Include a prominent safety note advising users not to handle unknown spiders with bare hands and to seek medical help immediately for severe reactions or concerning bites.

Integrate Spiders into the pest selection UI across EducationalContent tabs (Identify/Habitats/Prevention/Treatment) so users can select “Spiders” and view the new Spiders educational content, consistent with how Cockroaches and Head Lice are integrated.

Add a static spider icon image under frontend/public/assets/generated and use it in the Spiders selection control, consistent with the Cockroaches/Head Lice selection control pattern (use an tag with appropriate alt text).

Extend the Photo Scanner UI to provide spider-specific photo-based guidance when a user wants help identifying a spider from a captured/uploaded image: add an in-scanner “Spider Photo Help” section that explains how to take a useful spider photo and provides a simple, non-automated checklist to compare visible features (e.g., body shape, leg length/positioning, markings) plus next-step guidance (safe removal, monitoring, when to call a professional). Do not claim automated identification or use AI/ML classification.

Add an optional one-click action in the Photo Scanner flow to tag the current saved scan with “spider” (or “spiders”) when the user is using spider help, leveraging the existing saved-scan tags system and keeping all behavior local to the browser.

Add a new educational content component for “Moths & Larvae” with clear, user-facing English guidance covering: (1) basic identification cues and common signs (including fabric/pantry damage and shed skins/webbing where relevant), (2) where they are commonly found indoors and what attracts them, (3) prevention steps, and (4) what to do if found (safe clean-up, monitoring, and when to call a professional).

Add a new educational content component for “Beetles” with clear, user-facing English guidance covering: (1) basic identification cues and common signs of indoor beetle activity, (2) common indoor sources (e.g., stored foods, textiles, incidental outdoor entry) and what attracts them, (3) prevention steps, and (4) what to do if found (clean-up, storage fixes, monitoring, and when to call a professional).

Add a new educational content component for “Wood Beetles” with clear, user-facing English guidance covering: (1) basic identification cues and common signs (e.g., small exit holes, fine powdery frass, damaged/weakening wood), (2) where they are commonly found indoors and typical sources (structural wood, furniture, firewood), (3) prevention steps, and (4) what to do if found (isolation steps, documentation, monitoring, and when to contact a licensed professional).

Add a new educational content component for “Black Stink Bugs” with clear, user-facing English guidance covering: (1) basic identification cues and common signs (including odor when disturbed), (2) how and why they enter homes and where they hide, (3) prevention steps (exclusion/entry-point sealing), and (4) what to do if found (safe removal methods that avoid crushing, cleanup tips, and when to call a professional).

Integrate “Moths & Larvae”, “Beetles”, “Wood Beetles”, and “Black Stink Bugs” into the pest selection UI in frontend/src/components/EducationalContent.tsx across the Identify, Habitats, Prevention, and Treatment tabs, consistent with the existing pattern used for Cockroaches/Head Lice/Spiders (selection buttons + conditional rendering of the selected pest’s component).

Add static icon image assets for “Moths & Larvae”, “Beetles”, “Wood Beetles”, and “Black Stink Bugs” under frontend/public/assets/generated and use them in the pest selection controls (via tags with appropriate alt text) consistent with existing pest icon usage (e.g., cockroach/head-lice/spider icons).

Add four new ant educational content components for: Black Ants, Red Ants, Army Ants (interpret user text "arm ants" as "army ants"), and Carpenter Ants. Each component must use clear, user-facing English text and follow the existing pest content layout patterns (cards + alerts) to cover: (1) basic identification cues and common signs, (2) where they are commonly found and what attracts them, (3) prevention steps, and (4) what to do if found (safe clean-up/control steps, monitoring, and when to call a professional). Include a prominent safety note about stings/allergic reactions where relevant, and include a prominent structural-damage caution for Carpenter Ants.

Integrate Black Ants, Red Ants, Army Ants, and Carpenter Ants into the pest selection UI in frontend/src/components/EducationalContent.tsx across the Identify, Habitats, Prevention, and Treatment tabs, consistent with the existing pattern (selection buttons + conditional rendering via renderPestContent).

Add static icon image assets for Black Ants, Red Ants, Army Ants, and Carpenter Ants under frontend/public/assets/generated and use them in the pest selection controls via tags with appropriate alt text, consistent with existing pest icon usage (e.g., cockroach/head-lice/spider icons).

Add a new Earwigs educational content component that follows the existing pest content layout patterns (cards + alerts) and provides clear, user-facing English guidance covering: (1) basic identification cues and common signs indoors, (2) where earwigs are commonly found and what attracts them (especially moisture), (3) prevention steps, and (4) what to do if found (safe clean-up/control steps, monitoring, and when to call a professional).

Integrate Earwigs into the pest selection UI in frontend/src/components/EducationalContent.tsx across the Identify, Habitats, Prevention, and Treatment tabs, consistent with the existing pattern (selection button in the grid + conditional rendering via the selectedPest switch).

Expand the existing Spiders educational content (frontend/src/components/pests/SpidersContent.tsx) to include clearer, more detailed guidance specifically for Black Widow spiders, Brown Recluse spiders, and Wolf spiders. Add distinct subsections that include identification cues, typical indoor/outdoor hiding spots, safety/medical cautions, and what to do if found (including when to call a professional). Keep all user-facing text in English and consistent in tone with the existing Safety Warning/Medical Attention callouts.

Add and use a new static Earwig icon image asset under frontend/public/assets/generated, and use it in the Earwigs selection control via an tag with appropriate alt text, consistent with existing pest icon usage (e.g., cockroach/head-lice/spider icons).

new_feature_requests.summary: Add a cross-platform photo scanner feature that works on all phones (capture/upload photos for scanning).
Expand the app’s information content beyond bed bugs to include other bugs/pests (e.g., scorpions and mice).
Update the backend type model to support categorizing content beyond bed bugs (e.g., add categories for scorpions and mice) so future quizzes/guides can be fetched consistently by section/category.
Add photo management to the Photo Scanner: include a delete/trash icon for removing unwanted photos and an optional saved-photo gallery/backup for previously scanned images.
Add an option to reorder photos in the saved-photo gallery (change the order of saved scans).
Add a delete option in the saved-photo gallery UI so users can remove saved scans (with appropriate confirmation to prevent accidental deletion).
Add simple tagging for saved scans/photos to make it easier to organize and find items in the saved-photo gallery.
Add a "high heat treatment" icon to the bed bug issues/icons section.
Add educational content for “high heat treatment” that covers prep work homeowners should do before heat is applied to the home.
Add a checklist toggle for the Heat Treatment room preparation checklist so users can mark prep tasks as completed.
Add a simple reminder/confirmation step before starting the Heat Treatment room preparation checklist.
Add a prep-flow reminder/warning advising users not to throw out furniture or beds (to prevent bed bugs from hitchhiking and infesting other homes), using clear English user-facing text.
Add an additional safety/prep warning in the Heat Treatment checklist reminder/prep flow to remove firearms and ammunition and any other items that could explode before heat is applied.

Add a Heat Treatment safety/prep warning/step stating that only the heat treatment technician is allowed in the home while it is being heat treated (clear English user-facing text, consistent with existing checklist/reminder style).
Add a “quick tip” icon for the Bed Bugs section (static icon asset for use in the UI).
Add a cockroach icon as a static image asset for the pests/issues/icons set and use it in the UI.
Add educational content for cockroaches covering how to exterminate and help prevent cockroach infestations (clear English user-facing text).
Add educational content for head lice, including clear prevention guidance (user-facing text in English).
Add Spiders as a new pest category with educational content focused on prevention and what to do if you find a spider (clear, user-facing English guidance).
Integrate Spiders into the pest selection UI across EducationalContent tabs (and include a spider icon asset for the selection control, consistent with other pests).
Extend the Photo Scanner flow to support spider photo-based identification guidance (users can upload/capture a spider photo and get in-app help on identifying and how to get rid of them).
Add moths/larvae as new pest categories with educational content (identification cues, signs, prevention, and what to do if found).
Add beetles (including wood beetles) as new pest categories with educational content (identification cues, signs, prevention, and what to do if found).
Add black stink bugs as a new pest category with educational content (identification cues, signs, prevention, and what to do if found).
Integrate moths/larvae, beetles/wood beetles, and black stink bugs into the pest selection UI across EducationalContent tabs (consistent with existing pest integration).
Add ants as new pest categories (black ants, red ants, army ants, and carpenter ants) with educational content (identification cues, signs, prevention, and what to do if found).
Integrate ant categories (black ants, red ants, army ants, and carpenter ants) into the pest selection UI across EducationalContent tabs (consistent with existing pest integration).
Add earwigs as a new pest category with educational content (identification cues, signs, prevention, and what to do if found) in clear, user-facing English.
Integrate Earwigs into the pest selection UI across EducationalContent tabs (consistent with existing pest integration) and include an earwig icon asset for the selection control.
Expand the Spiders educational content to also include clear guidance for black widow spiders, brown recluse spiders, and wolf spiders (ID cues, safety notes, and what to do if found).
